tickets.description:
## Objective
Create the frontend package manifest with React, TypeScript, Vite, and router dependencies.

## Files to write

### frontend/package.json
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite --host 0.0.0.0",
    "build": "tsc && vite build",
    "preview": "vite preview --host 0.0.0.0"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "latest",
    "vite": "latest",
    "typescript": "latest",
    "react": "latest",
    "react-dom": "latest",
    "react-router-dom": "latest",
    "@types/react": "latest",
    "@types/react-dom": "latest"
  },
  "devDependencies": {}
}
```
tickets.worker_done_criteria: frontend/package.json exists with React and Vite dependencies.
tickets.qa_done_criteria: npm can read the package manifest.

constraints: Use relative frontend fetch URLs; proxy /api and /uploads.
tickets.worker_done_criteria: vite.config.ts exports defineConfig with proxy entries.

```

tickets.description:
## Objective
Define Pydantic models matching the frontend interfaces.

## Files to write

### backend/schemas.py
```python
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import List, Optional

class Category(BaseModel):
    id: int
    name: str
    sort_order: int

class CategoryIn(BaseModel):
    name: str
    sort_order: int = 0

class MenuItem(BaseModel):
    id: int
    name: str
    description: str
    price: int
    category_id: int
    category: str
    photo_url: str
    available: bool
    sort_order: int

class MenuItemIn(BaseModel):
    name: str
    description: str
    price: int
    category_id: int
    photo_url: str = ''
    available: bool = True
    sort_order: int = 0

class OrderItem(BaseModel):
    id: int
    order_id: int
    item_id: int
    item_name: str
    item_price: int
    quantity: int

class Order(BaseModel):
    id: int
    order_number: str
    customer_name: str
    status: str
    created_at: str
    updated_at: str
    items: List[OrderItem]

class CreateOrderItem(BaseModel):
    item_id: int
    quantity: int

class CreateOrder(BaseModel):
    customer_name: str
    items: List[CreateOrderItem]

class StatusUpdate(BaseModel):
    status: str

class Settings(BaseModel):
    cart_name: str
    tagline: str
    is_open: bool
    estimated_wait_minutes: int
    admin_pin: str

class PinCheck(BaseModel):
    pin: str
```
constraints: Pydantic fields must match TypeScript interfaces.
tickets.worker_done_criteria: schemas.py imports BaseModel and defines models.

tickets.description:
## Objective
Create SQLite connection helpers and schema initialization.

## Files to write

### backend/database.py
```python
import sqlite3
from pathlib import Path

BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
DB_PATH = BASE_DIR / 'foodcart.db'
UPLOAD_DIR = BASE_DIR / 'uploads'
UPLOAD_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

def get_db():
    conn = sqlite3.connect(DB_PATH)
    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
    return conn

def row_to_dict(row):
    return dict(row) if row is not None else None

def init_db():
    db = get_db()
    db.executescript('''
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS categories (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
    );
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS menu_items (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      description TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
      price INTEGER NOT NULL,
      category_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
      photo_url TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
      available INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
      sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      FOREIGN KEY(category_id) REFERENCES categories(id)
    );
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS orders (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      order_number TEXT NOT NULL,
      customer_name TEXT NOT NULL,
      status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'received',
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    );
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS order_items (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      order_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
      item_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
      item_name TEXT NOT NULL,
      item_price INTEGER NOT NULL,
      quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
      FOREIGN KEY(order_id) REFERENCES orders(id)
    );
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS settings (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK (id = 1),
      cart_name TEXT NOT NULL,
      tagline TEXT NOT NULL,
      is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
      estimated_wait_minutes INTEGER NOT NULL,
      admin_pin TEXT NOT NULL
    );
    ''')
    db.commit()
    db.close()
```
constraints: Use sync sqlite3 only. get_db sets row_factory.
qa_cmd: cd backend && python -c "from database import init_db; init_db(); print('ok')"

tickets.description:
## Objective
Seed categories, menu items, and default settings.

## Files to write

### backend/seed.py
```python
from database import get_db, init_db

PHOTOS = [
    'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1565299624946-b28f40a0ae38',
    'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1550547660-d9450f859349',
    'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1565958011703-44f9829ba187',
    'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1551504734-5ee1c4a1479b'
]

def seed():
    init_db()
    db = get_db()
    if db.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) c FROM categories').fetchone()['c'] == 0:
        cats = [('Tacos', 1), ('Bowls', 2), ('Sides', 3), ('Drinks', 4)]
        db.executemany('INSERT INTO categories (name, sort_order) VALUES (?,?)', cats)
    if db.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) c FROM menu_items').fetchone()['c'] == 0:
        items = [
            ('Carnitas Taco','Slow pork, salsa verde, onion, cilantro',450,1,PHOTOS[0],1,1),
            ('Mushroom Taco','Roasted mushrooms, crema, pickled onion',425,1,PHOTOS[0],1,2),
            ('Chicken Tinga Taco','Smoky chicken, cotija, lime',475,1,PHOTOS[0],1,3),
            ('Street Corn Bowl','Rice, beans, corn, crema, chili lime',1050,2,PHOTOS[2],1,1),
            ('Barbacoa Bowl','Braised beef, rice, beans, salsa roja',1250,2,PHOTOS[1],1,2),
            ('Chips & Guac','House chips with fresh guacamole',650,3,PHOTOS[3],1,1),
            ('Elote Cup','Sweet corn, crema, cotija, tajin',550,3,PHOTOS[3],1,2),
            ('Agua Fresca','Rotating fruit agua fresca',400,4,PHOTOS[2],1,1)
        ]
        db.executemany('''INSERT INTO menu_items
        (name,description,price,category_id,photo_url,available,sort_order) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?)''', items)
    db.execute('''INSERT OR IGNORE INTO settings
    (id,cart_name,tagline,is_open,estimated_wait_minutes,admin_pin)
    VALUES (1,'Sunset Taco Cart','Fresh street food, made fast',1,12,'1234')''')
    db.commit()
    db.close()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    seed()
```
tickets.worker_done_criteria: seed.py exports seed and can run as script.

tickets.description:
## Objective
Implement category CRUD API routes.

## Files to write

### backend/routers/categories.py
```python
from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
from database import get_db, row_to_dict
from schemas import CategoryIn

router = APIRouter(prefix='/api/categories', tags=['categories'])

@router.get('')
def list_categories():
    db = get_db()
    rows = db.execute('SELECT * FROM categories ORDER BY sort_order, name').fetchall()
    db.close()
    return [row_to_dict(r) for r in rows]

@router.post('')
def create_category(data: CategoryIn):
    db = get_db()
    cur = db.execute('INSERT INTO categories (name, sort_order) VALUES (?,?)', (data.name, data.sort_order))
    db.commit()
    row = db.execute('SELECT * FROM categories WHERE id=?', (cur.lastrowid,)).fetchone()
    db.close()
    return row_to_dict(row)

@router.patch('/{category_id}')
def update_category(category_id: int, data: CategoryIn):
    db = get_db()
    db.execute('UPDATE categories SET name=?, sort_order=? WHERE id=?', (data.name, data.sort_order, category_id))
    db.commit()
    row = db.execute('SELECT * FROM categories WHERE id=?', (category_id,)).fetchone()
    db.close()
    if not row:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail='Category not found')
    return row_to_dict(row)

@router.delete('/{category_id}')
def delete_category(category_id: int):
    db = get_db()
    used = db.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) c FROM menu_items WHERE category_id=?', (category_id,)).fetchone()['c']
    if used:
        db.close()
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail='Category has menu items')
    db.execute('DELETE FROM categories WHERE id=?', (category_id,))
    db.commit()
    db.close()
    return {'ok': True}
```
constraints: Call get_db directly; do not use Depends.
qa_cmd: cd backend && python -c "from routers.categories import router; print('ok')"

tickets.description:
## Objective
Implement menu item browse and admin CRUD API routes.

## Files to write

### backend/routers/menu.py
```python
from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
from database import get_db, row_to_dict
from schemas import MenuItemIn

router = APIRouter(prefix='/api/menu', tags=['menu'])

SELECT_SQL = '''SELECT m.*, c.name as category FROM menu_items m
JOIN categories c ON c.id = m.category_id'''

def as_item(row):
    data = row_to_dict(row)
    data['available'] = bool(data['available'])
    return data

@router.get('')
def list_menu(available_only: bool = True):
    db = get_db()
    sql = SELECT_SQL + (' WHERE m.available=1' if available_only else '') + ' ORDER BY c.sort_order, m.sort_order, m.name'
    rows = db.execute(sql).fetchall()
    db.close()
    return [as_item(r) for r in rows]

@router.get('/{item_id}')
def get_menu_item(item_id: int):
    db = get_db()
    row = db.execute(SELECT_SQL + ' WHERE m.id=?', (item_id,)).fetchone()
    db.close()
    if not row:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail='Item not found')
    return as_item(row)

@router.post('')
def create_menu_item(data: MenuItemIn):
    db = get_db()
    cur = db.execute('''INSERT INTO menu_items
    (name,description,price,category_id,photo_url,available,sort_order) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?)''',
    (data.name,data.description,data.price,data.category_id,data.photo_url,int(data.available),data.sort_order))
    db.commit()
    row = db.execute(SELECT_SQL + ' WHERE m.id=?', (cur.lastrowid,)).fetchone()
    db.close()
    return as_item(row)

@router.patch('/{item_id}')
def update_menu_item(item_id: int, data: MenuItemIn):
    db = get_db()
    db.execute('''UPDATE menu_items SET name=?, description=?, price=?, category_id=?,
    photo_url=?, available=?, sort_order=? WHERE id=?''',
    (data.name,data.description,data.price,data.category_id,data.photo_url,int(data.available),data.sort_order,item_id))
    db.commit()
    row = db.execute(SELECT_SQL + ' WHERE m.id=?', (item_id,)).fetchone()
    db.close()
    if not row:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail='Item not found')
    return as_item(row)

@router.delete('/{item_id}')
def delete_menu_item(item_id: int):
    db = get_db()
    db.execute('DELETE FROM menu_items WHERE id=?', (item_id,))
    db.commit()
    db.close()
    return {'ok': True}
```
qa_cmd: cd backend && python -c "from routers.menu import router; print('ok')"

tickets.description:
## Objective
Implement cart settings API routes.

## Files to write

### backend/routers/settings.py
```python
from fastapi import APIRouter
from database import get_db, row_to_dict
from schemas import Settings

router = APIRouter(prefix='/api/settings', tags=['settings'])

@router.get('')
def get_settings():
    db = get_db()
    row = db.execute('SELECT cart_name,tagline,is_open,estimated_wait_minutes,admin_pin FROM settings WHERE id=1').fetchone()
    db.close()
    data = row_to_dict(row)
    data['is_open'] = bool(data['is_open'])
    return data

@router.put('')
def update_settings(data: Settings):
    db = get_db()
    db.execute('''UPDATE settings SET cart_name=?, tagline=?, is_open=?,
    estimated_wait_minutes=?, admin_pin=? WHERE id=1''',
    (data.cart_name,data.tagline,int(data.is_open),data.estimated_wait_minutes,data.admin_pin))
    db.commit()
    row = db.execute('SELECT cart_name,tagline,is_open,estimated_wait_minutes,admin_pin FROM settings WHERE id=1').fetchone()
    db.close()
    result = row_to_dict(row)
    result['is_open'] = bool(result['is_open'])
    return result
```
qa_cmd: cd backend && python -c "from routers.settings import router; print('ok')"

constraints: GET /api/orders returns an array directly. No Depends.
tickets.worker_done_criteria: orders.py exports router and websocket endpoints.
qa_cmd: cd backend && python -c "from routers.orders import router; print('ok')"

tickets.description:
## Objective
Implement admin PIN and upload API routes.

## Files to write

### backend/routers/auth.py
```python
from fastapi import APIRouter
from database import get_db
from schemas import PinCheck

router = APIRouter(prefix='/api/auth', tags=['auth'])

@router.post('/pin')
def check_pin(data: PinCheck):
    db = get_db()
    row = db.execute('SELECT admin_pin FROM settings WHERE id=1').fetchone()
    db.close()
    return {'ok': bool(row and data.pin == row['admin_pin'])}
```

### backend/routers/uploads.py
```python
from fastapi import APIRouter, UploadFile, File, HTTPException
from pathlib import Path
from uuid import uuid4
from database import UPLOAD_DIR

router = APIRouter(prefix='/api/uploads', tags=['uploads'])

@router.post('')
async def upload_image(file: UploadFile = File(...)):
    if file.content_type not in ['image/jpeg','image/png','image/webp','image/gif']:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail='Image file required')
    content = await file.read()
    if len(content) > 5 * 1024 * 1024:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail='Max image size is 5MB')
    ext = Path(file.filename or 'image.jpg').suffix or '.jpg'
    name = f'{uuid4().hex}{ext}'
    path = UPLOAD_DIR / name
    path.write_bytes(content)
    return {'url': f'/uploads/{name}'}
```
constraints: Upload returns single object directly with url field.
qa_cmd: cd backend && python -c "from routers.auth import router as a; from routers.uploads import router as u; print('ok')"

constraints: Persist cart under localStorage key foodcart_cart.

tickets.worker_done_criteria: Layout exports default and money helper.

constraints: Use cart context only for cart state.
POST /api/orders and parse order object directly.
Use multipart POST /api/uploads for photo upload.

tickets.description:
## Objective
Wire the FastAPI app, routers, CORS, static uploads, seeding, and frontend static serving.

## Files to write

### backend/main.py
```python
from pathlib import Path
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles
from fastapi.responses import FileResponse
from database import init_db, UPLOAD_DIR
from seed import seed
from routers import categories, menu, settings, orders, auth, uploads

app = FastAPI(title='Food Cart API', redirect_slashes=False)

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=['http://localhost:5173','http://127.0.0.1:5173','http://localhost:8000','http://127.0.0.1:8000'],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=['*'],
    allow_headers=['*'],
)

init_db()
seed()

app.include_router(categories.router)
app.include_router(menu.router)
app.include_router(settings.router)
app.include_router(orders.router)
app.include_router(auth.router)
app.include_router(uploads.router)

app.mount('/uploads', StaticFiles(directory=str(UPLOAD_DIR)), name='uploads')

FRONTEND_DIST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / 'frontend' / 'dist'

@app.get('/api/health')
def health():
    return {'ok': True}

@app.get('/{full_path:path}')
def serve_frontend(full_path: str):
    index = FRONTEND_DIST / 'index.html'
    requested = FRONTEND_DIST / full_path
    if requested.exists() and requested.is_file():
        return FileResponse(requested)
    if index.exists():
        return FileResponse(index)
    return {'message': 'Frontend not built. Run npm run build in frontend.'}
```
constraints: Use FastAPI redirect_slashes=False. Include all routers.
tickets.worker_done_criteria: main.py creates app and includes all routers.
qa_cmd: cd backend && python -c "from main import app; print(app.title)"

constraints: Use BrowserRouter, Routes, and Route from react-router-dom v6.
tickets.worker_done_criteria: App.tsx exports default App with all routes.
qa_cmd: cd frontend && npm install && npm run build
